title: Policy Underwriting Decision Audit Trail
dataSources: `index=insurance` `sourcetype="underwriting:audit"` (policy_id, decision_id, user_id, decision, risk_score, rule_id, epoch)
app: Policy administration / underwriting engine via HEC
spl:
index=insurance sourcetype="underwriting:audit"
| eval bind=if(lower(decision) IN ("bind","approved","accept"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as decisions, sum(bind) as binds, dc(rule_id) as rules_fired by user_id, _time
| eval bind_ratio=round(100*binds/nullif(decisions,0),2)
| where decisions > 50 AND bind_ratio > 85 AND rules_fired < 2
| table _time, user_id, decisions, binds, bind_ratio, rules_fired
description: Immutable-style audit of quote, risk tier, and bind decisions supports regulatory exams and disputes; Splunk supplements the system of record for search and dashboards.
value: Immutable-style audit of quote, risk tier, and bind decisions supports regulatory exams and disputes; Splunk supplements the system of record for search and dashboards.
implementation: Ingest append-only decision events with tamper-evident hashing if required by compliance. Tune alert for unusual auto-approval patterns; investigate false positives with underwriting leadership. Not a replacement for GRC workflow. **Domain context:** Model governance (SR 11-7 style expectations for larger insurers) requires explainability—`rule_id` diversity alone is a coarse anomaly signal. **Splunk:** Restrict index to compliance roles; hash/lineage often lives in the policy admin system of record.
visualization: Timeline (decision volume), Table (suspicious users), Bar chart (bind ratio by rule).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Policy administration / underwriting engine via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=insurance` `sourcetype="underwriting:audit"` (policy_id, decision_id, user_id, decision, risk_score, rule_id, epoch).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest append-only decision events with tamper-evident hashing if required by compliance. Tune alert for unusual auto-approval patterns; investigate false positives with underwriting leadership. Not a replacement for GRC workflow. **Domain context:** Model governance (SR 11-7 style expectations for larger insurers) requires explainability—`rule_id` diversity alone is a coarse anomaly signal. **Splunk:** Restrict index to compliance roles; hash/lineage often lives in the policy admin system of re…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=insurance sourcetype="underwriting:audit"
| eval bind=if(lower(decision) IN ("bind","approved","accept"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as decisions, sum(bind) as binds, dc(rule_id) as rules_fired by user_id, _time
| eval bind_ratio=round(100*binds/nullif(decisions,0),2)
| where decisions > 50 AND bind_ratio > 85 AND rules_fired < 2
| table _time, user_id, decisions, binds, bind_ratio, rules_fired
```

Understanding this SPL

**Policy Underwriting Decision Audit Trail** — Immutable-style audit of quote, risk tier, and bind decisions supports regulatory exams and disputes; Splunk supplements the system of record for search and dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=insurance` `sourcetype="underwriting:audit"` (policy_id, decision_id, user_id, decision, risk_score, rule_id, epoch). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Policy administration / underwriting engine via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: insurance; **sourcetype**: underwriting:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=insurance, sourcetype="underwriting:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bind** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bind_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where decisions > 50 AND bind_ratio > 85 AND rules_fired < 2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Policy Underwriting Decision Audit Trail**): table _time, user_id, decisions, binds, bind_ratio, rules_fired


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (decision volume), Table (suspicious users), Bar chart (bind ratio by rule).
grandmaExplanation: Immutable-style audit of quote, risk tier, and bind decisions supports regulatory exams and disputes; our monitoring platform supplements the system of record for search and dashboards — so the things customers and regulators care about stay running and accountable.
